case.title: FARBWERKE VORMALS MEISTER LUCIUS & BRUNING v. CHEMICAL FOUNDATION

opinion.h: FARBWERKE VORMALS MEISTER LUCIUS & BRUNING v. CHEMICAL FOUNDATION, 282 U.S. 819 (1930)
FARBWERKE vormals MEISTER LUCIUS & BRUNING et al., etc., petitioners, v. CHEMICAL FOUNDATION, Inc., et al. No. 179.
FARBWERKE vormals MEISTER LUCIUS & BRUNING, petitioner, v. E. I. DU PONT DE NEMOURS & CO. et al. No. 180.
DEUTSCHE GOLD & SILBER SCHEIDE ANSTALT vormals ROESSLER, petitioner, v. E. I. DU PONT DE NEMOURS & CO. et al. No. 181.
BADISCHE-ANILIN & SODA-FABRIK, petitioner, v. E. I. DU PONT DE NEMOURS & CO. et al. No. 182.
Walter O. WOODS, as Treasurer, etc., petitioner, v. The CHEMICAL FOUNDATION, Inc., et al. No. 271.
Howard SUTHERLAND, as Alien Property Custodian, petitioner, v. FARBWERKE vormals MEISTER LUCIUS & BRUNING et al. No. 272.
Howard SUTHERLAND, as Alien Property Custodian, petitioner, v. DEUTSCHE GOLD & SILBER SCHEIDE ANSTALT vormals ROESSLER et al. No. 273.
Howard SUTHERLAND, as Alien Property Custodian, petitioner, v. BADISCHE ANILIN & SODA-FABRIK et al. No. 274.
opinion.p: Messrs. Frederick H. Wood and Almuth C. Vandiver, both of New York City, for petitioners Farbwerke vormals and others.
The Attorney General, for petitioners Woods and Sutherland.
Mr. G. Carroll Todd, of Washington, D. C., for respondents.
Petitions for writs of certiorari to the United States Circuit Court of Appeals for the Third Circuit granted.
